tool_design.role_in_intervention: Bridge tool between routine monitoring and full excursion response.
tool_design.usage_moment: Immediately after a trigger reading or recurring warning trend
tool_design.expected_output: Logged trigger event with first response and escalation decision

failure_warnings.warning: Entries are logged late or inconsistently.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: Patterns cannot be reviewed or escalated.

failure_warnings.warning: Tracker is updated but never used to decide action.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: Information is stored but not acted on.

content:
# Temperature Excursion Trigger Log **Tool ID:** T001-03 **Block:** B001 (Daily Temperature Monitoring) **Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety) **Level:** L2 **Format:** Tracker **Role:** Shift Manager / Manager **Time Required:** 5-12 minutes per event **Version:** 2.0 --- ## What This Tool Is For Use this the moment a daily monitoring check finds a unit outside its safe limit. This is the bridge between routine monitoring in `B001` and full incident handling in `B003`. Use this log to capture: - what reading triggered concern - what immediate checks were done - whether food was still safe, moved, or discarded - whether the issue was minor drift or a true excursion event - whether a full incident report in `T003-02` was triggered --- ## Trigger Thresholds | Unit Type | Trigger Point | Immediate Response | |---|---|---| | Chilled fridge | `8C` or above | Protect stock, alert manager, record food temperatures, decide whether `B003` response is required | | Freezer | `-12C` or warmer | Keep door shut, assess stock, alert manager, record action | | Repeated warning trend | Same unit above normal range 3 times in 7 days | Log as an equipment warning even if food stayed safe | --- ## Immediate Action Checklist
  • I recorded the actual reading from [T001-01].
  • I checked for simple causes first: door, plug, airflow, overload, frost.
  • I probed at least one high-risk product where relevant.
  • I notified the manager or duty lead.
  • I decided whether this stays a local corrective action or moves into [T003-02].
--- ## Trigger Log Form **Date:** ____________________ **Time discovered:** ____________________ **Found by:** ____________________ | Field | Details | |---|---| | Unit name | | | Unit type | Fridge / Freezer / Display / Other | | Reading found | | | Safe range for that unit | | | Product checked | | | Product temperature | | | Likely cause seen at first check | Door open / Overloaded / Power / Fault / Unknown | | Manager notified at | | | Stock moved? | Yes / No | | Stock discarded? | Yes / No | | Unit back in range by | | | Full B003 incident report opened? | Yes / No | ### Action Notes __________________________________________________ __________________________________________________ __________________________________________________ ### Stock Decision | Stock Category | Decision | Notes | |---|---|---| | Milk / dairy | Keep / Use soon / Discard | | | Ready-to-eat fillings | Keep / Use soon / Discard | | | Desserts / pastries | Keep / Use soon / Discard | | | Frozen stock | Keep / Transfer / Discard | | ### Sign-Off **Closed by:** ____________________ **Date / time:** ____________________ --- ## When To Escalate Into B003 Open the full excursion process if any of these are true: - chilled food reached `8C` or above and duration is unclear - freezer stock warmed to `-12C` or above - the unit did not recover after immediate checks - stock had to be discarded - the cause suggests equipment failure rather than a simple user error --- ## Pattern Watch If the same unit appears in this log more than once in a month, raise it in [T001-04]. Repeated "small" drifts often show the story before a full breakdown happens. --- ## Connected Resources **Used with:** - T001-01 - Daily Temperature Log & Check Sheet - T001-04 - Monthly Temperature Compliance Dashboard - T001-05 - Temperature Monitoring SOP & Team Sign-Off - T003-02 - Excursion Incident Report Form **Related blocks:** - B003 - Temperature Excursion Response --- *Tool Version 2.0 - small enough for the moment, serious enough for the record.*
